title: Alnylam — Projects
html:
Module 5: Active Projects & Workstream Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Alnylam account has 15+ active workstreams across three therapeutic areas, with ATTR/AMVUTTRA accounting for the majority of project volume and complexity. The account is in growth mode driven by the ATTR-CM launch, with new study types (PCA for combination therapy, cardiologist-specific ATU panels, strategy studies) layering onto established tracking programs. Three performance themes cut across the portfolio: (1) AMVUTTRA reps deliver strong impact per detail but face a critical cardiologist reach gap that limits overall market penetration; (2) US message effectiveness is showing signs of fatigue (sharp declines in Q4 2024) that may presage similar trends internationally; and (3) the rare disease programs (GIVLAARI, OXLUMO) are performing well promotionally but growth is constrained by diagnosis gaps and access barriers, not by messaging deficits.

Account Health Dashboard

Workstream Status Last Deliverable Key Finding
AMVUTTRA US PET (CM) :green_circle: Active Q4 2024 wave All manufacturers declining reach; BridgeBio only one growing. AMVUTTRA message effectiveness declined 10-31 pts across messages -- fatigue signal
AMVUTTRA US PET (PN) :green_circle: Active Semi-annual wave Stable program; PN is mature market
AMVUTTRA ATU (ATTR-CM) :green_circle: Active Q1 2025 Integration 14/39 evaluable cardiologists are "stabilizer only users" -- key conversion target
AMVUTTRA ATU (hATTR-PN) :green_circle: Active One wave (80 physicians) Smaller study; PN market fully penetrated by RNAi
AMVUTTRA PCA :green_circle: Active Q2 2026 delivery (upcoming) Combination therapy charts emerging: AMVUTTRA + VYNDAMAX (24), AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY (16)
International PET (France) :green_circle: Active Q2 2024 (W2, neuros only) 100% Alnylam neuro reach; 55% total SOV. Strong ONPATTRO loyalty persists
International PET (Italy) :yellow_circle: Watch Q2 2024 (W2) AMVUTTRA reach declined 58% to 51%; both products' NPS declined; competitive noise growing
International PET (Germany) :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2024 (W2) Near-parity (47% vs 53% SOV). Price sensitivity flagged (~$25M quarterly revenue reduction from German price cuts)
International PET (Spain) :green_circle: Strong Q4 2024 (W2) Strongest market: SOV flipped from 35% to 56%. Reach hit 91%. The success template
International PET (Japan) :yellow_circle: Watch Q4 2024 (W2) Pfizer-dominant (59% SOV, 84% reach). AMVUTTRA impact is strong when reps reach physicians -- reach gap, not message gap
Patient Message Recall :green_circle: Active [Check timing] Tracks patient-level message transfer for ATTR
AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy :blue_circle: Ad hoc 2025 Strategic imperatives mapping to research deliverables
Integrated Analysis :green_circle: Active Q1 2025 Integration ADOPT funnel framework integrating PET+ATU+PCA
GIVLAARI US PET :yellow_circle: Stale Q2 2023 Last PET data is 3+ years old. Disease perception has shifted significantly since (ATU W4 shows chronic reframing). PET refresh needed
GIVLAARI Global ATU :green_circle: Active Wave 4 (Sep-Oct 2024) HCP segmentation crystallized: 47% Inexperienced segment is the growth opportunity. Chronicity narrative gaining traction in US/Germany, not Brazil
OXLUMO PET :green_circle: Active Q3 2025 Final Report OXLUMO reach dropped from 81% to 47% as Rivfloza entered. OXLUMO maintains quality lead but Rivfloza reps catching up on perception change
OXLUMO ATU :blue_circle: New Wave 1 (Dec 2024) First full ATU. Nephrologist-urologist divergence confirmed. Access perception gap is the most consistent finding
Check-In Meetings :green_circle: Ongoing 04.29.2026 22+ meetings; weekly cadence. Deep partnership signal

Legend: :green_circle: Active/Strong | :yellow_circle: Watch (declining metric or gap) | :red_circle: Risk | :blue_circle: New/Ad hoc

Detailed Workstream Profiles

ATTR / AMVUTTRA Programs

AMVUTTRA US PET (ATTR-CM)

Status: Active | Latest: Q4 2024 wave | Cadence: Quarterly

What's working:

  • Comprehensive tracking across 4 manufacturers (Alnylam, Pfizer, BridgeBio, AstraZeneca) -- 90 interactions/product/quarter
  • AMVUTTRA reps drive strong perception improvement when reaching physicians
  • Cross-study integration with ATU and PCA provides adoption funnel intelligence

Watch items:

  • All manufacturers showed declining reach and frequency QoQ (Q4 2024): Alnylam -1pt, Pfizer -7pts, AstraZeneca -4pts; BridgeBio +5pts (only one growing)
  • AMVUTTRA message effectiveness declined 10-31 points across nearly all messages -- a concerning fatigue signal
  • Access/affordability message O23 showed sharpest decline (-29 pts on differentiation)

Key questions for next readout:

  • Is the message effectiveness decline a one-quarter blip or a sustained trend?
  • How is ATTRUBY's rapid uptake (7,800+ prescriptions by Feb 2026) affecting AMVUTTRA's competitive position?
  • What is the cardiologist-specific performance? Does the neurologist-cardiologist gap persist in US data?

AMVUTTRA ATU (ATTR-CM)

Status: Active | Latest: Q1 2025 Integration | Cadence: Quarterly (90 cardiologists/wave)

What's working:

  • New HCP segmentation: AMV users (32-57%), Likely TTR Rxers (4-11%), Stabilizer only (39-57%)
  • "Stabilizer only" segment identified as the conversion target -- 14 of 39 evaluable cardiologists

Watch items:

  • Survey length at 26 minutes, projected to increase to 30-33 minutes (MaxDiff + Wainua loop addition). Approaching 35-minute upper limit
  • New target list reduced available respondents by 56% for ATU, 72% for PCA

AMVUTTRA PCA (Patient Chart Audit)

Status: Active | Latest: Q2 2026 delivery upcoming | Cadence: Semi-annual

What's working:

  • Only patient-level view of treatment patterns -- tracks the ADOPT funnel at the prescribing level
  • Captures combination therapy patterns (silencer + stabilizer): AMVUTTRA + VYNDAMAX (24 CM charts), AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY (16 CM charts)
  • 300 CM+Mixed charts + 100 PN charts per wave

Watch items:

  • New target list reduced available PCA respondents by 72% -- significant recruitment challenge
  • Combination therapy chart share (~15% of CM charts currently) is a leading indicator of treatment paradigm shift. Watch for inflection above 30%
  • User type distribution: 67-72% current users, 22% non-users, 6-7% non-treaters. The 22% non-user segment is the active conversion target

International PET (5 Countries)

Status: Active | Latest: Wave 2 completed across all markets | Cadence: Semi-annual

Country Performance Summary:

Country AMVUTTRA SOV (W2) Trend from W1 Key Dynamic Priority
Spain 56% Up from 35% Best market. SOV flipped. 91% reach. Template for other markets :green_circle: Reference
France 55% (total Alnylam) Stable Neuro-dominant. ONPATTRO loyalty. Not yet capturing cardiology :yellow_circle: Expand
Germany 47% Improving toward parity Price-sensitive. ~$25M quarterly revenue hit from price cuts :yellow_circle: Watch
Italy ~40% (est.) Declining reach (58% to 51%) Both products' NPS declined. Increasing competitive noise :yellow_circle: Watch
Japan 41% Stable trailing Pfizer-dominant (59% SOV, 84% reach). AMVUTTRA impact strong when reached (78% perception improvement) :yellow_circle: Invest

Cross-market pattern: The neurologist vs. cardiologist divide is the universal finding. AMVUTTRA SOV among cardiologists trails neurologists by 12-34 points across all markets. The "perception paradox" (cardiologists who see AMVUTTRA details are highly persuaded -- problem is reach, not receptivity) is the single most actionable cross-market insight.

Integrated Analysis

Status: Active | Latest: Q1 2025 Integration | Cadence: Quarterly

The highest-value deliverable in the ATTR program. Integrates PET (what reps say), ATU (what physicians believe), and PCA (what physicians actually do) into the ADOPT funnel framework: Awareness > Depth of Knowledge > Overall Product Perception > Product Usage > Treatment Advocacy.

Each study feeds different funnel stages, and the integration reveals disconnects (e.g., high awareness but low usage, strong perception but no prescribing action) that single-study readouts miss.

Rare Disease Programs

GIVLAARI US PET

Status: Stale (last data Q2 2023) | Latest: Q2 2023 | Cadence: Semi-annual

What's working (at last measurement):

  • All GIVLAARI messages rated above rare disease industry average for effectiveness
  • Over 50% of sales calls are "high-impact details"
  • NPS trending upward; reps rated above industry standards on all attributes

Critical gap:

  • Last PET data is from Q2 2023 -- over 3 years ago. The ATU Wave 4 (Oct 2024) captured a fundamental shift in disease perception (episodic to chronic) that the PET has not measured
  • The Converter segment (22% of HCPs, high patient load, low GIVLAARI share) requires specific messaging data that only PET can provide, but the PET predates the segmentation
  • A refreshed PET that incorporates the chronicity narrative and tests updated messaging is needed

Recommendation: Propose a GIVLAARI PET refresh as a BD opportunity. Frame it as: "The disease perception has shifted since your last PET. Your reps may still be leading with attack prevention messages while physicians are now thinking about chronic symptom management. A refreshed PET validates whether your field force has adapted."

GIVLAARI Global ATU (US, Germany, Brazil)

Status: Active | Latest: Wave 4 (Sep-Oct 2024) | Cadence: Annual

What's working:

  • HCP segmentation is actionable: Enthusiasts (11%), Trialists (21%), Converters (22%), Inexperienced (47%)
  • Disease chronicity reframing captured -- majority of US HCPs now view AHP as chronic condition
  • Cross-country comparison reveals structural differences (US: access barrier; Germany: highest share; Brazil: stalled)

Watch items:

  • Brazil share expected to remain flat (~26%) -- structural access problem, not a commercial one. ~45% of diagnosed patients not on consistent treatment
  • The Inexperienced segment (47%) is the largest but hardest to convert -- they default to hemin-as-needed not from rejection but from payer-driven thresholds
  • Patient Types 4 (chronic symptoms) and 5 (biochemically active) are the expansion frontier but payer coverage is uncertain

OXLUMO PET

Status: Active | Latest: Q3 2025 Final Report | Cadence: Semi-annual

What's working:

  • Head-to-head OXLUMO vs. Rivfloza competitive tracking established
  • OX1 (quarterly SC, all ages, all kidney stages) is most recalled (66%) AND most effective (ME 70%)
  • OXLUMO maintains higher overall sales call quality (83% vs. Rivfloza)

Watch items:

  • OXLUMO reach dropped from 81% (Q4 2023 baseline) to 47% (Q3 2025) -- Rivfloza split the share of voice
  • Rivfloza reps catching up on perception change metrics (driven by novelty)
  • Rivfloza self-administration message (RI9) is the single most effective message across BOTH brands (ME up to 90%)
  • OX2 (advanced CKD/dialysis data) and OX3 (largest trial program) are underleveraged: 38% recall but 66-69% ME -- delivery gap

OXLUMO ATU

Status: New (Wave 1) | Latest: Dec 2024 | Cadence: TBD (likely annual)

What's working:

  • First comprehensive ATU for PH1 -- establishes baseline across disease beliefs, treatment patterns, and product perceptions
  • Nephrologist-urologist divergence clearly documented
  • Hypothetical patient profiles reveal specialty-specific treatment decision-making

Watch items:

  • Urologists significantly less familiar with PH1 treatments beyond supportive care and transplant -- a major education gap
  • Access/affordability perception gap (Rivfloza perceived as more affordable even without experience) confirmed in ATU, consistent with PET data
  • Small samples (60 HCPs total) inherent to ultra-rare disease -- confidence intervals should be flagged

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. The Cardiologist Conversion Challenge (ATTR)

Across every ATTR data source -- US PET, international PET (5 countries), ATU, and PCA -- the neurologist-cardiologist divide is the dominant finding. AMVUTTRA dominates among neurologists and trails among cardiologists by 12-34 SOV points. Given that ATTR-CM is the growth driver (200,000 US patients vs. ~50,000 globally for hATTR-PN) and cardiologists control the majority of prescribing, closing this gap is Alnylam's single highest-impact commercial lever. ZoomRx's data uniquely quantifies this gap across multiple countries and dimensions.

Implication: Every ATTR deliverable should include cardiologist-specific analysis, even when not explicitly requested. This is the lens through which Alnylam leadership evaluates market progress.

2. Message Fatigue in a Crowding Market (ATTR US)

US AMVUTTRA message effectiveness declined 10-31 points across nearly all messages in Q4 2024. This is the most concerning trend in the US program. Contributing factors likely include: market saturation from repeated messaging, competitive noise from ATTRUBY and Wainua launches, and need for message refresh. The access/affordability message showed the sharpest decline, suggesting competitors are eroding AMVUTTRA's access perception.

Implication: Watch whether this Q4 2024 decline is a leading indicator that presages similar international trends as competitive noise increases globally (ATTRUBY/BEYONTTRA launching ex-US in 2026-2027).

3. The Safety Trap Persists (ATTR, All Markets)

A universal rep behavior pattern across all international markets: safety messages are the most recalled but the least effective; the rapid TTR knockdown narrative is the most motivating but recalled in only 33-50% of details. This finding has been consistent across every wave and every country.

Implication: This is a message optimization opportunity that ZoomRx should repeat in every ATTR readout until the rep behavior changes. The recommendation is simple and actionable: shift message mix from safety to rapid knockdown efficacy.

4. Promotional Effectiveness Is Not the Ceiling for Rare Disease (AHP + PH1)

Both GIVLAARI and OXLUMO show strong promotional metrics -- messages above industry average, reps rated highly, NPS trending up. Yet growth in both TAs is constrained by non-promotional factors: diagnosis delay (15 years in AHP), access barriers (#1 barrier in PH1), and ultra-small patient pools. The most productive strategic lever for Alnylam is not message optimization -- it is expanding who gets diagnosed and who gets access.

Implication: ZoomRx's strategic value in rare disease goes beyond PET readouts. Propose study types that address the real growth bottlenecks: diagnostic journey studies, patient identification pathway research, access/payer studies, and urologist education modules.

Immediate Priorities

  1. Monitor Q1 2026 US AMVUTTRA PET for message effectiveness recovery. The Q4 2024 decline across all messages is the most urgent signal. If it persists, recommend formal message refresh to Alnylam.

  2. Track PCA combination therapy chart share. Currently ~15% of CM charts show silencer + stabilizer combinations. If this rises above 30%, it signals a paradigm shift that changes AMVUTTRA's positioning from "replacement" to "foundational therapy."

  3. Prepare for CARDIO-TTRansform data readout (mid-2026). This is the single biggest upcoming market event for ATTR. If positive, Wainua PET fielding resumes and creates silencer-vs-silencer competition. If negative, AMVUTTRA's position strengthens dramatically. Either outcome requires rapid study adaptation.

  4. Propose GIVLAARI PET refresh. The 3+ year gap between the last PET (Q2 2023) and the disease perception shift captured in ATU Wave 4 (Oct 2024) creates a blind spot. This is both a data gap and a BD opportunity.

  5. Address the new target list recruitment challenge. The 56-72% reduction in available respondents affects ATU and PCA directly. Proactively develop methodology bridges and alternative recruitment strategies before fielding deadlines hit.

  6. Surface the OXLUMO access perception gap to Alnylam leadership. This is the most consistent and actionable finding across 2 years and 4 studies. Frame it as: "Novo Nordisk is winning the access perception game without data to back it up. Your access story is better than HCPs think."

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
